axioms.name: Principles over Rules
axioms.statement: Anxious, inexperienced writers obey rules. Rebellious, unschooled writers break rules. Artists master the form.

axioms.name: The Master Law
axioms.statement: You must not tell the audience what to think or feel. You must show and let them discover it themselves.

axioms.name: The Law of Conflict
axioms.statement: Nothing moves forward in a story except through conflict. All change is expressed through value shifts.

axioms.name: Archetype vs Stereotype
axioms.statement: Archetypes reveal deep human truth; stereotypes merely repeat surface conventions. Use the former, avoid the latter.

levels.name: STORY
levels.description: Overarching unit containing all acts and the primary spine

levels.name: ACT
levels.description: Major structural division culminating in a major reversal

levels.name: SEQUENCE
levels.description: Series of scenes building toward a moderate reversal

levels.name: SCENE
levels.description: Action unit unified by time/place turning on a value change

levels.name: BEAT
levels.description: Smallest unit of behavior in action/reaction exchange

core_algorithms.name: The Gap Algorithm
core_algorithms.purpose: Generate narrative tension through the difference between expectation and result
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. Character takes action; 2. Character expects result Y; 3. World responds with result Z (Z != Y); 4. GAP opens; 5. Character escalates risk; REPEAT until Story Climax;

core_algorithms.name: Character Revelation Protocol
core_algorithms.purpose: Reveal true character through choice under pressure
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. Establish characterization (surface); 2. Apply pressure; 3. Character makes choice; 4. IF choice != mask: REVEAL deep character; 5. ESCALATE pressure for deeper revelation;

core_algorithms.name: Controlling Idea Discovery
core_algorithms.purpose: Identify the thematic meaning emerging from the Story Climax
core_algorithms.pseudocode: final_value = story_climax.closing_value; cause = identify_cause(story_climax); RETURN f'{final_value} is achieved because {cause}';

core_algorithms.name: Scene Validity Test
core_algorithms.purpose: Ensure every scene contributes a structural value shift
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF scene.opening_value == scene.closing_value: RETURN NON_EVENT (REJECT); ELSE: RETURN VALID_SCENE;

diagnostic_questions.question: Does every scene contain a value turn (opening value != closing value)?
Is there a clear 'Gap' between character expectation and world response?
Does the character's choice under pressure contradict their surface characterization?
Do the forces of antagonism equal or exceed the protagonist's capacities?
Is the climax a true dilemma between irreconcilable goods or lesser evils?
Is exposition converted into ammunition in present conflict?

key_constraints: Nothing moves forward except through conflict
Coincidence only to start trouble, never to resolve it
No hugging, no learning (for the cynical end of the triangle)
